controls.title: User-set passwords are at least 12 characters
Anti-automation controls resist credential attacks (rate limiting, lockout)
Passwords stored using an approved one-way key derivation function
Passwords, integrations and API secrets are not stored in source code; a secrets store is used
A new session token is generated on user authentication
Logout and expiration invalidate the session token
Cookie-based session tokens set the Secure attribute
Cookie-based session tokens set the HttpOnly attribute
The principle of least privilege is enforced by access controls
Sensitive data and APIs are protected against Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
A strong anti-CSRF mechanism protects authenticated functionality
Frameworks protect against mass parameter assignment
eval() and other dynamic code execution features are avoided
The application protects against template injection attacks
The application protects against SSRF attacks
Output encoding is relevant for the interpreter and context required
Context-aware output escaping protects against reflected, stored, and DOM XSS
Database queries use parameterized queries, ORMs, or are otherwise protected from database injection
Where parameterized mechanisms are not possible, context-specific output encoding protects against injection
The application protects against LDAP injection
The application protects against OS command injection
The application protects against local and remote file inclusion (LFI/RFI)
The application protects against XPath and XML injection
XML parsers are restricted to the most restrictive configuration (XXE protection)
Deserialization of untrusted data is avoided or protected
Regulated private data is stored encrypted at rest
Industry-proven or government-approved cryptographic algorithms are used
Insecure block modes, padding modes, small-block ciphers, and weak hashing algorithms are not used
Random values are generated with an approved cryptographically secure RNG
A secrets management solution (key vault) is used to create, store, and control secrets
The application does not log credentials or payment details
Security-relevant events are logged
Logging components encode data appropriately to prevent log injection
TLS is used for all client connectivity
Connections to and from the server use trusted TLS certificates (validation enforced)
Code integrity protections (signing, subresource integrity) are employed
User-submitted filenames are protected against path traversal
All components are up to date and free of known vulnerabilities
frame-ancestors / X-Frame-Options protects against clickjacking
The CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin header uses a strict allow-list

notAssessable.title: Architecture, Design and Threat Modeling
notAssessable.reason: Requires design review and threat modeling, not code scanning.

notAssessable.title: Data Protection
notAssessable.reason: Data classification, retention, and handling are process controls invisible to static analysis.

notAssessable.title: Business Logic
notAssessable.reason: Business-logic abuse requires understanding intended behavior; static rules cannot assess it.

notAssessable.title: API and Web Service
notAssessable.reason: API behavior verification requires runtime testing (DAST), which this platform does not yet run.
